response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and systems software engineer with strong web, backend, and developer-tooling credentials, but his profile has essentially zero overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, deep-learning workload performance analysis, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear anywhere in this resume. His closest brush with performance work is low-latency HTTP services and async I/O in DLang, which is categorically different from silicon-level performance modeling. His educational background (CS + Fine Arts) also falls short of the hardware engineering depth the role expects, and there is no evidence of GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or DNN inference optimization experience.
strengths: Long tenure with measurable impact across multiple senior/staff-level roles, demonstrating sustained technical growth and delivery.
Low-latency and high-throughput systems experience at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second using async I/O and fiber-based DLang) shows some performance-engineering sensibility.
Recent AI-adjacent work at Naver on MCP code generators and agentic infrastructure indicates awareness of the AI tooling landscape.
concerns: No SoC, IP modeling, or hardware architecture experience of any kind — the core technical requirement of this role is entirely absent.
No background in deep-learning workload profiling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator optimization, which are the explicit required and preferred qualifications.

Education and career trajectory are firmly in web/full-stack software; there is no hardware-software co-design work, no chip-level performance analysis, and no prior collaboration with silicon architects.

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or GPU architecture background
